data.title: Coinbase Stock (COIN) Faces Senate Review on Stablecoin Rewards
data.description:
Coinbase Global (COIN), the largest crypto exchange in the U.S., is monitoring a major crypto bill as it approaches Senate review, according to a Bloomberg report.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the center of the debate is whether crypto platforms should be allowed to continue offering rewards on stablecoin holdings. Coinbase views those rewards as a core part of its platform and is concerned that new limits under discussion could disrupt that model.

Reportedly, Coinbase may reconsider its support for the bill if the final verdict includes strict limits on rewards. The legislation is expected to face markup in at least one Senate committee this week. However, Coinbase has not issued any public response yet.

Banks and Crypto Firms Take Opposite Sides

Some lawmakers are considering rules that would allow only banks or licensed financial firms to offer stablecoin rewards. Banks support this idea, saying that rewards could pull deposits out of the traditional banking system and reduce lending.

Crypto firms, including Coinbase, oppose the move. They argue that restricting rewards to banks would give traditional lenders an unfair edge and make it harder for crypto platforms to compete.

Stablecoin Rewards Drive a Key Revenue Stream

Stablecoin rewards are a meaningful income source for Coinbase. The company earns interest from reserves backing USDC, a dollar-linked stablecoin issued by Circle (CRCL). When users hold USDC on Coinbase, that interest provides steady income, even when trading slows.

To support those balances, Coinbase offers rewards of about 3.5% on certain USDC holdings. If new rules curb those incentives, users may move stablecoins elsewhere.

That could pressure results. Stablecoin-related revenue is expected to reach about $1.3 billion in 2025, based on Bloomberg data, making it an important part of Coinbase's business.

Is COIN Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on Coinbase Global stock based on 17 Buys, seven Holds, and one Sell recommendation. The average COIN stock price target of $361.63 indicates 50.19% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) Places $22 Million Lead Bid for Luminar Assets
data.description:
Quantum Computing Inc. (QUBT) said it has been picked as the stalking horse bidder for select assets of Luminar Technologies Inc. (LAZRQ) as part of Luminar's Chapter 11 process. The proposed bid is worth about $22 million and will run through a court-led auction where other buyers can still step in.
If approved by a U.S. court and no higher bid wins, the deal is set to close in the first quarter of 2026. At the same time, this move is part of a much larger plan.
Earlier, QCI agreed to buy Luminar Semiconductor Inc. in an all-cash deal valued at about $110 million.
That deal is also undergoing a court review because the chip unit is owned by Luminar.

Meanwhile, QUBT shares dropped 1.84% in Friday, closing at $11.72.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Digital Custody Firm BitGo Aims to Raise $201M in IPO Despite Crypto Wobbles
data.description:
Digital asset custody firm BitGo is looking to raise around $201 million from its upcoming U.S. IPO.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to an SEC filing, the Palo Alto, California-based company founded in 2013 ‍is offering 11.8 million ‍shares ​priced between $15 and $17 each.

Potential $1.75B Valuation

Goldman Sachs (GS) and Citigroup (C) are ‌the lead underwriters for the offering. BitGo intends to list on the New York ​Stock Exchange under the symbol "BTGO". It has been reported that the company is aiming for a first quarter listing with a potential valuation of $1.75 billion.

Unlike speculative token startups or meme-coin exchanges, BitGo built its brand on custody, compliance, and infrastructure—and has become a favorite of banks, asset managers, and institutional investors wanting exposure to crypto without the operational risk. Its clients include exchanges, hedge funds, and firms managing billions in digital assets.

Crypto is Wobbling

When BitGo confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the middle of last year ,the global crypto market was rocketing forward. However, in recent weeks, the prices of currencies such as Bitcoin have slid as a result of fears over an AI bubble, interest rate uncertainty and a general risk-off sentiment among investors.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Abercrombie Stock Hits a Bump as Management Says Growth Wasn't Fast Enough to Raise Profit Forecast
data.description:
The red-hot streak for Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF) hit a major bump on Monday. Shares of the clothing retailer sank by as much as 17% in early trading following a business update that left investors wanting more. Even though the company reported record sales through December, the growth wasn't fast enough to convince management to raise their future profit targets. This sudden reality check comes after a long period where the brand seemed like it could do no wrong in the eyes of the market.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It Was a Cautious Mood in the Shopping Aisles

The main issue isn't that the company is failing, but that it is growing more slowly than people expected. Abercrombie now predicts that sales for the final quarter of the year will grow by about 5%. This number sits right in the middle of their previous goal, which disappointed traders who were betting on a breakout holiday season.

While back-to-school shopping was strong, many customers became more careful with their money as the winter holidays approached. This shift in spending has forced the company to be more conservative as it looks ahead to the rest of 2026.

Costs and Competition Create New Hurdles

Aside from the sales numbers, the company is facing rising costs that are starting to eat into its profits. New tariffs on imported goods and higher shipping fees are expected to cost the retailer roughly $90 million this year. Because of these extra expenses, the company had to slightly lower its expectations for its total profit margins. At the same time, fast-fashion rivals are fighting harder for the same young shoppers, making it more expensive for Abercrombie to keep its spot as a trend leader.

Looking Toward a More Stable Path

Even with today's stock drop, the company is still in a much better position than it was a few years ago. Both the Abercrombie and Hollister brands are still popular with Gen Z and Millennial shoppers, and the company is sitting on a solid pile of cash.

The goal now is to focus on steady, profitable growth instead of trying to chase unsustainable viral trends. As the team prepares for its next big financial report in March, they will be looking for ways to keep customers coming back without having to resort to the deep discounts that usually hurt a brand's image.

Is Abercrombie & Fitch a Good Stock to Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, ANF stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on four Buys and five Hold ratings assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month ANF price target sits at $115.25, implying a 7.7% downside risk.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: XYZ, SOFI, PYPL Rally: Analyst Sees Trump's 10% Credit Interest Cap Boosting Fintech
data.description:
Shares of personal loan providers and buy now, pay later (BNPL) companies Block (XYZ), SoFi Technologies (SOFI), PayPal (PYPL), Affirm (AFRM), and Upstart (UPST) are rallying in pre-market trading. Mizuho Securities analyst Dan Dolev argues these sectors are poised to benefit from President Donald Trump's proposed 10% cap on credit card interest rates. The policy could pressure credit card giants like Visa (V), Mastercard (MA), American Express (AXP), and Capital One Financial (COF), whose shares dipped on the news.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump posted the idea on his Truth Social (DJT) account on Friday, leaving unclear whether it would advance through executive order or new legislation. A Republican senator confirmed he had spoken with Trump, who fully backs a bill and wants it ready by January 20, exactly a year after he took office.

One Company's Loss Is Another's Gain

Trump claims that credit card rates of 20%-30% are "ripping off" Americans, and thus, vows to cap them at 10%, starting January 20. This would halve typical borrowing costs from the current roughly 20% average APR. At the same time, it could prompt banks to tighten lending to low-credit-score customers.

According to Dolev, this shift could drive borrowers toward alternatives like BNPL firms like Affirm, Block, PayPal, and personal loan providers like SoFi and Upstart, some of which are particularly focused on the lower-FICO customer. The New York Fed estimates that the U.S. has $1.2 trillion in credit card balances, with Dolev estimating over 50% of consumers holding FICO scores below 745 and facing higher interest rates.

Dolev noted that lower-FICO consumers face tighter restrictions on traditional credit cards, boosting demand for alternatives. This creates strong volume growth opportunities and upside potential for credit-heavy names, translating to net revenue if they underwrite and select new borrowers effectively.

Which Is the Best Fintech Stock, According to Analysts?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which stock among those mentioned above, analysts are currently most bullish on. Analysts have assigned a "Strong Buy" consensus rating to Block stock, with a 25.6% upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Fall on Tensions between Trump and Fed's Powell
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were down on Monday morning as investors reacted to the news that U.S. President Donald Trump has subpoenaed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.72%, 0.52%, and 0.64%, respectively, at 8:45 a.m. EST on January 12.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Friday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed higher, with the S&P 500 and the Dow Jones closing at record highs. Despite a volatile trading week, the indexes ended in positive territory. The S&P 500, the Dow Jones, and the Nasdaq Composite gained over 1%, 2.3%, and 1.9%, respectively.

Looking ahead, investors will closely watch key economic reports, including the important Consumer Price Index (CPI) data due on Tuesday, Producer Price Index (PPI) data, retail sales, and weekly initial jobless claims.

Moreover, the fourth-quarter earnings season is set to open, with major banks reporting this week. Notably, JPMorgan (JPM), Delta Air Lines (DAL), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Goldman Sachs (GS), and Morgan Stanley (MS) are scheduled to announce their results this week.

Turning to trending stocks, Sun Country Airlines (SNCY) rallied 17% in Monday's pre-market trading on news of its acquisition by Allegiant Travel (ALGT) in a $1.5 billion cash and stock deal. Also, Walmart (WMT) stock gained 3% after the Nasdaq said that the retail giant will be added to the Nasdaq-100 index (NDX) later this month. Inclusion in this popular index could drive more demand. The Invesco QQQ Trust ETF (QQQ) tracks the Nasdaq 100. Also, the retailer announced its partnership with Alphabet-owned Google (GOOGL) to enhance the consumer shopping experience by leveraging AI.

Meanwhile, bank and financial stocks were trending lower after Trump called for a 10% cap on credit card interest rates for one year.

data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: Cathie Wood's ARK Makes New Biotech Bets in Her Latest Trades — Here's What It Sold
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made several portfolio moves across biotech and industrial names on Friday, January 9, 2026. The firm increased its exposure to early-stage biotech stocks, while trimming a major genomics name.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Which Stocks Did ARK Buy on January 9?

On the buying side, ARK's largest move was a fresh add to Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA). The firm bought 216,411 shares, worth about $2.28 million, across its ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG).

The purchase highlights ARK's continued confidence in gene-editing technology, even as the biotech sector remains volatile.

Building on that, ARK also added 262,769 shares of Personalis Inc. (PSNL) through its ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG). The buy, valued at roughly $2.20 million, follows earlier purchases in the week, pointing to steady accumulation.

Outside biotech, ARK increased exposure to industrial equipment by buying 49,053 shares of Komatsu Ltd. (KMTUY), worth about $1.59 million, via its ARK Space Exploration & Innovation ETF (ARKX).

data.description:
